Hey, it's Phil with MBL 2.0. In this video, we are going to step through installing the MBL 2.0 launch sequence, the main launch sequence, into your active campaign account. So we have provided you with an importable link that will actually provide you with the entire structure of what you're going to need to stitch this together. And so what you're gonna wanna do when you go into your active campaign account, simply click this blue button top right to create an automation. And then you're gonna select the import automation and you're gonna paste the link from this course into this automation URL and click the import button. And this might take a minute or two for active campaign to process through. What it's going to do is bring up the automation setup wizard and you'll click the blue button again to get started. And the first thing it's going to have you do is go through and set your own from name and from email for each of these individual emails. So you'll want to set those for yourself and then just kind of breeze through. It's gonna do this on each of the emails. I think this sequence has 10 emails. It's gonna have you do that 10 times. So I'm just gonna quickly step through that so you can see what's going on here. And almost there. And at this point, if we do a command shift R to refresh, that should be the only thing that active campaign asks of you to get started. So now that we have this in place, I just want to step you through some of the important aspects you're going to need to have a baseline understanding of to make this work for you. So you already have your workbook that has the MVL 2.0 structure. I have followed this main day one through day seven sequence. This automation that I've provided you with includes everything minus the day X1A and day X1B. Those are the triggered emails and you will have separate automation imports for those as well as another video where I'll step you through those. But everything else I have essentially mapped out and obviously these are customizable. So what I've done is I've just applied some thinking to timing of this sequence. You could certainly run with this as a starting point or modify as what feels good for you and your audience. And so the way this is set up, you will always start your automation with a trigger tag. And so I go really deep into this idea of tag taxonomy and AI and kind of a lot more about how I keep things organized. But you can, you know, without having to understand all the big picture, you can do this yourself. So basically you have a trigger tag, which will be the name of your sequence and you want to be consistent with that. So that's what you see here. I've used as the name of my kind of sequence as sales dash MVL 2.0. So yours, let's say your product was like, we have a product automated intimacy. So if we were setting up the sale sequence for automated intimacy, it would be trigger colon sales dash automated intimacy. Right? So that's the trigger. And then I'm going to use that consistently. So that tag when applied will put a contact into the sequence. And so when you go to pull your list of contacts that you want to send this campaign to, you're just going to do a bulk update where you add this tag to each of those contacts, which will then start the automation. Then we remove the trigger tag because we want to keep things clean. We add a tag that's called historical tag that tells us that they've actually been through the sequence. So that's the sales colon MVL 2.0. If we were doing automated intimacy, again, it would be sales colon automated intimacy. And then we use what's called a hotel tag, which lets us know that someone is actively in this automation. So that's where you see sales MVL 2.0 in. That just tells us we've checked them into the automation. So anytime you see an intake on a contact, you know they're actively in this sequence. And then for this hypothetical, what we've done, sorry, something, I'm sitting outside here on a farm and something just kind of flew up in my neck there. So I've got this set to start at 8 a.m. on a Monday. So this particular launch, this hypothetical launch would go Monday through Sunday. You can modify this. Basically what you're doing here is you're setting the day of the week and the time of the week that you want to wait for this to start. And then from that point on, everything else is relative with hours. And so you could see after 8 a.m. on Monday, we send email one, we wait for 24 hours. And now the next thing we do before we send every email is we're going to check for two things. We're gonna check for a mute tag. So the mute tag that you apply, this is when in some, several of the emails, so in the pre-launch as well as the mid, let's see, the mid launch check-in, a few other spots, we allow people to basically say, hey, I want to get out of here. I don't want to continue to get these emails. You're gonna use the mute tag link action function to create that experience. And I will provide you with another video around that topic. We're also going to filter out anyone who has the purchase tag. So we would suggest that whatever cart system you're using, we like Thrive Cart, when the purchase is made, apply a very specific tag for that product. So in this case, it's perch colon MBL 2.0. If it was automated intimacy, it would be perch colon automated intimacy. So all we're doing here is saying, if someone has muted out, or they've already made the purchase, we don't want to continue to send them through this sequence. And so we're just going to, in that case, we're gonna remove that in tag and we're gonna add the out tag. So we know they cleared the automation and we're gonna end the automation. And so we're gonna do that before every single email that we send. So you see, we're doing that before day two, we're waiting another 24 hours, we're doing that same check. And we just continue to do that all the way through this sequence. And so I've lined this up exactly for you following your workbook. Now, this is the structure. The emails are not in there. The assumption is you've gotta write your own emails, right? And everybody has their own format to how they wanna structure these emails within the campaign builder. So all I've done is create kind of the structure for you. But all you'll do is, like, if you were going to build the mid-card temperature check, you would just edit this, open it up, and then you would start to build out your email based on your own copy doc following the MVL 2.0 templates. And so that is essentially it for this sequence. This will take you all the way through day seven, something you'll see here at the end, which is optional. I always put a wait timer of at least 14 days at the end of a sales sequence because as you very well probably know, launches can be dynamic and adaptive. And we may decide we wanna add something on to the very end. Maybe this is a down sell or a referral opportunity or some new addition or way that we're offering it. And if they've completely exited the automation, now we've gotta go back and find those people within our full list. So what I like to do is just leave this last wait tag. So it gives me a little bit of time to do a debrief or post launch analysis and decide if there's anything I wanna kind of bolt on to the end of the sequence to maximize any returns or anything like that. So that is essentially it. The last piece of what you'll need to fully implement MVL 2.0 is to import the automations for the triggered emails as well as watch the quick video that I'll create for you that will help you understand how those actually work. All right, much love. Hope you're having a beautiful day. Take care.
